summary:
There is no direct evidence demonstrating the effectiveness of daily chlorhexidine mouthwash use up to two weeks against *Streptococcus mutans*. Existing studies focus on broader antimicrobial properties or non-oral uses, failing to establish validated efficacy or duration specifics related to oral pathogen control within such a timeframe.  

Final answer: None of the above.
